intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE - cross-version check of the critical floor-blind-spot case. Same rscons.cil type-attribute constraint scenario as rocky9-constraint-matching-context, replayed against el8 (policyvers 31). Uses a privileged container with bind-mount to install rscons.cil via semodule -n (semodule cannot rename within the overlayfs without --privileged bind-mount to tmpfs). El8 result: Constraint DENIED, same bucket as el9.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds}; floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected}. Same as rocky9: authoritative=Constraint, floor=none (floor blind spot confirmed on el8 too).

oracle.audit2why_detail: constrain process { signal } ((t2 == rscons_exempt -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED
oracle.custom_policy_note: rscons.cil installed via semodule -n (no reload) into a bind-mounted tmpfs copy of the module store, then policy.cons.el8 dumped from /var/lib/selinux/targeted/active/policy.kern. The oracle uses policy.cons.el8, not stock policy.31.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Type-attribute constraint violation: a constrain on process signal requires the target to carry the rscons_exempt attribute, which rscons_tgt_t lacks. A TE allow cannot fix a constraint.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A floor-only impl sees matching role (system_r) and matching level (s0), finds nothing to flag, and SUGGESTS the narrow allow - which is WRONG (the constraint blocks it regardless). Only an impl that consults the authoritative policy layer gets this right. Confirmed on both el8 and el9.

xver_baseline.policyvers_note: The type-attribute constraint semantics (constrain ... (eq t2 <attr>)) are enforced identically by libsepol on policyvers 31 and policyvers 33. The Constraint bucket is stable across el8/el9.
xver_baseline.toolchain_note: semodule -n with --privileged bind-mount is required on el8 containers because overlayfs cross-device rename fails without it. The rocky9 scenario ran on a live VM where semodule worked natively.
